description: Also known as Vitamin B3, it is a water-soluble vitamin that works with the natural substances in your skin to help visibly improve enlarged pores, uneven skin tone, fine lines and wrinkles, dullness, and a weakened surface.
clinical_notes: Clinical studies show 5% niacinamide can significantly reduce hyperpigmentation and increase skin lightness after 4 weeks of use.

description: A beta hydroxy acid that exfoliates the skin. It is oil-soluble, allowing it to penetrate deep into the pores to dissolve dead skin cells and sebum.
clinical_notes: Highly effective for comedonal acne. Avoid combining with retinol or AHAs in the same routine to prevent severe irritation.

description: A naturally occurring glycosaminoglycan found throughout the body's connective tissue. It is a powerful humectant that can hold up to 1000 times its weight in water.
clinical_notes: Must be applied to damp skin and sealed with a moisturizer/emollient to prevent trans-epidermal water loss (TEWL).

description: A potent antioxidant that neutralizes free radicals, aids in your skin's natural regeneration process, and helps repair damaged skin cells.
clinical_notes: Highly unstable. Must be stored in a dark, airtight container. Best used in the morning under sunscreen.

description: A derivative of Vitamin A, it is the gold standard in anti-aging skincare. It increases cell turnover and stimulates collagen production.
clinical_notes: Causes 'purging' and irritation initially. Must be used at night only, and requires daily broad-spectrum SPF 30+.

description: Lipids (fats) that are found naturally in high concentrations in the uppermost layers of skin. They make up over 50% of skin's composition.
clinical_notes: Essential for repairing a damaged skin barrier caused by over-exfoliation or harsh acne treatments.

description: An herb used in traditional Asian medicine. It contains active compounds like madecassoside that possess powerful healing and anti-inflammatory properties.
clinical_notes: Excellent for post-inflammatory erythema (red acne marks) and soothing skin after laser treatments or chemical peels.
